text: Dealing with confrontation Intros | have always been someone who goes out of their way to help. | have had very good feedback from clinic and ward nurses. They hold me in high regard Negative: However, there are times when | have been taken advantage of, and as a result end up with larger workloads and losing out on opportunities to grow because | was not confident in confronting the issue. Example: A particular example that comes to mind one term, my colleagues would ask for help inthe clinic, and then leave early to go to the theatre and leave me in the clinic alone. This resulted in me having more jobs to follow up from the clinc and less theatre time. In the end, | raised this issue privately with them and we resolved it Reflect: | felt | had let myself down in this instance, and it made me realise how crucial teamwork s. Although it is good to help your colleagues, respect in a team must flow both ways. And as part of this, communicating with each other is vital Lessons: | have become very aware of my difficulty in raising issues with colleagues. | have undertaken a course and now use the CUSS graded assertiveness method to raise these issues in order to avoid them
